doom.driver: Putin hails Russia's test launch of a new ballistic missile.
doom.blurb: PUTIN FIRES WORLD'S MOST POWERFUL MISSILE. FOR FUN.
doom.summary: Russia decided today was a great day to test-fire a new intercontinental ballistic missile — the Sarmat, which Putin is calling the world's most powerful — while Trump is literally on a plane to Beijing to talk about a separate ongoing war with Iran. The timing is either a coincidence or a masterclass in geopolitical trolling. With nuclear modernization accelerating, a U.S.-Iran war in month three, Trump-Xi summit stakes at maximum, and UK bond markets in meltdown, today's chaos is impressively multi-threaded.

factors.name: Nuclear
factors.detail: Russia's Sarmat ICBM test-launch — entering combat service by year-end — is a deliberate signal of nuclear capability during a period of maximum geopolitical tension.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Trump arrives in Beijing for a summit with Xi as the Iran war enters day 75, with Gulf tensions rising, a UN resolution on Hormuz, and Taiwan and tariffs all on the table.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: UK Prime Minister Starmer faces potential ouster, causing gilts sell-off and bond market panic — democratic instability in a G7 nation is never a great look.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: Silicon Valley giants deepening war-contractor roles (Palantir, Anduril, Google) while Nvidia's Jensen Huang joins Trump's China trip — AI and geopolitics are now fully fused.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: A growing hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship (now 11 cases, one critically ill on an artificial lung) and a QR code conspiracy theory disrupting Georgia's election infrastructure are both quietly unsettling.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing — wide aerial view angled down at 45 degrees, camera looking steeply down at a vast flat steppe landscape rendered in deep navy shadow and smoldering orange haze, the enormous treeless terrain dominating the entire frame — at the symbolic center of the composition, a massive stone chessboard has been carved directly into the scorched earth itself, its alternating squares of dark graphite and pale concrete stretching across the barren ground like a scar, the board mid-collapse, the far edge of it cracking and tilting downward into a circular void in the earth, a perfectly cylindrical silo-shaped hole blasted open in the steppe below — heavy black smoke billows upward and outward from the void in a wide lateral skirt, catching the threatening orange warmth from below and turning the smoke columns deep amber and rust at their cores — the chess pieces themselves are colossal, monolithic, carved from the same dark gray stone as the board, and several have toppled, lying at angles across the cracking squares — the sky above is a suffocating deep navy, filtered through smoke haze, no horizon visible, just oppressive darkness pressing down — tiny anonymous figures stand at the very edge of the board's perimeter, dwarfed by the landscape, observing — smoldering orange rimlight edges every cracked stone surface, warmth feeling entirely threatening.

dawn.driver: Scientists make old blood stem cells young again in major anti-aging breakthrough.
dawn.blurb: SCIENTISTS TURN BACK THE CLOCK ON AGING BLOOD
dawn.summary: Researchers have identified how overactive lysosomes drive aging in blood stem cells — and found a way to calm them, effectively rejuvenating the immune system in older adults. This is the kind of foundational biology breakthrough that quietly rewrites the ceiling on human health, with implications for everything from cancer treatment to immune decline. Even as geopolitical headlines grind on, science moves forward on its own relentless, hopeful schedule.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: Reversing biological aging in blood stem cells represents a genuine mechanistic breakthrough, not just a correlation study — researchers identified and targeted a specific cellular pathway.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: Brazil's 27-year process to officially demarcate 410,000 hectares for the Kawahiva people finally begins, a rare win for indigenous land rights and legal persistence.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: Gaza sisters Farah and Tala turned rubble into reusable bricks, winning a prize and embodying resilience and ingenuity under unimaginable hardship.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: Scientists engineered algae that can magnetically bind and remove microplastics from drinking water, offering a low-cost, scalable path to cleaner water supplies globally.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: A commencement speaker at NC State paid off all graduating seniors' final-year student loans, delivering a genuinely debt-free start to hundreds of young lives.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. A fog-drenched hospital geriatric ward corridor at golden hour, seen from mid-distance, dense amber haze rolling in through tall windows along the left wall, the mist softening every edge into warm teal and gold gradients, long diffuse shadows stretching across pale linoleum tiles. The atmosphere is primary — thick, luminous fog fills the upper two-thirds of the frame, backlit by dying amber sun filtering through condensation-fogged glass panes. Within this weather-veiled scene: an elderly patient sitting upright in a bedside chair near the window, hands resting on knees, face tilted slightly upward toward the amber light breaking through the fog as if sensing something shift, a faint warm glow emanating from their chest in deep violet-purple hues suggesting cellular renewal within. Beside them on a rolling tray table, a small glass vial of faintly blue liquid catches the golden light. Through the fog-blurred window glass, the suggestion of a city beyond — soft teal outlines of buildings dissolving into haze. A single nurse pauses mid-corridor in the background, silhouetted against glowing window light, head slightly turned. The overall palette is deep amber, warm burgundy, and luminous teal, fog refracting every light source into soft radiant halos, quietly triumphant.
